ListItem.Para: APU is not used.
Left AGCU operates normally.
VFSG systems operate normally.
EDTO beyond 180 minutes is not conducted.

Note.Para: A VFSG system is considered to be operating normally if its associated status message (ELEC GEN SYS L1, L2, R1 or R2) is not displayed
An inoperative ASG may be removed provided dispatch tool K24012 is installed. If the APU is operated for more than 120 hours with an ASG removed, the APU gearbox should be inspected for wear.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate left and right APU Starter Generator (ASG) generators by positioning associated Electrical Panel (P5) APU GEN L and APU GEN R switches OFF.

Note.Para: Simultaneous engine start is not allowed.

ListItem.Para: EDTO beyond 180 minutes is not conducted
Start the first engine using the Ground Power Engine Start Supplementary Procedure.
Left ASG starter is deactivated.
APU battery operates normally.
APU SPU operates normally.
Right ASG generator operates normally.
Right ASG starter operates normally.
VFSG systems operate normally.
EDTO beyond 180 minutes is not conducted.

Note.Para: A VFSG system is considered to be operating normally if its associated status message (ELEC GEN SYS L1, L2, R1 or R2) is not displayed.
An inoperative ASG may be removed provided dispatch tool K24012 is installed. If the APU is operated for more than 120 hours with an ASG removed, the APU gearbox should be inspected for wear.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the left ASG starter (AMM DDG 24-22-02).

ListItem.Para: Position associated Electrical Panel (P5) APU GEN L switch OFF.
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker APU START EXCITATION L and lock with an INOP tag.

Note.Para: Simultaneous engine start is not allowed.
ELEC APU STARTER L status message may be displayed based on day rotation for APU starting with external power.
PACK L or PACK R advisory message may be displayed before engine start.

ListItem.Para: Flight must remain within 180 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added further (O) procedure to provide guidance on how to minimize PECS heating.

ListItem.Para: To minimize PECS heating, operators should consider the following options:
Set packs OFF and use pre-conditioned air prior to engine start.
Delay closing forward or aft cargo doors until airplane is ready for push back or engine start.
Minimize APU ground operations.
Start APU prior to closing cargo doors.
Minimize time between cargo door closing and first engine start.
Each engine should be started individually at the gate.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added further (O) procedure to provide guidance on how to minimize PECS heating.

ListItem.Para: On POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm SUPPLY TEMP of left and right PECS loop is 142 degrees F (61 degrees C) or lower.
APU battery operates normally.
APU SPU operates normally.
Right ASG generator operates normally.
Right ASG starter operates normally.

OperationNote.rev:revHighlight: Added (O) note to provide further dispatch clarification.
OperationNote.Para: On POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm SUPPLY TEMP of left and right PECS loop is 142 degrees F (61 degrees C) or lower.

ListItem.Para: Right ASG starter is deactivated.
Left AGCU operates normally.
Left ASG generator operates normally.
Left ASG starter operates normally.
VFSG systems operate normally.
Flight remains within 180 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.

Note.Para: A VFSG system is considered to be operating normally if its associated status message (ELEC GEN SYS L1, L2, R1 or R2) is not displayed.
An inoperative ASG may be removed provided dispatch tool K24012 is installed. If the APU is operated for more than 120 hours with an ASG removed, the APU gearbox should be inspected for wear.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the right ASG starter (AMM DDG 24-22-02).

ListItem.Para: Position associated Electrical Panel (P5) APU GEN R switch OFF.
Open P049 circuit breaker SPU HOLD ON CONTROL and lock with an INOP tag.

Note.Para: APU cannot be started using the APU battery.
ELEC APU START POWER UNIT status message may display during the APU start and remain displayed.
Simultaneous engine start is not allowed.
ELEC APU STARTER R status message may be displayed based on day rotation for APU starting with external power.
PACK L or PACK R advisory message may be displayed before engine start.

ListItem.Para: Flight must remain within 180 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added further (O) procedure to provide guidance on how to minimize PECS heating.

ListItem.Para: To minimize PECS heating, operators should consider the following options:
Set packs OFF and use pre-conditioned air prior to engine start.
Delay closing forward or aft cargo doors until airplane is ready for push back or engine start.
Minimize APU ground operations.
Start APU prior to closing cargo doors.
Minimize time between cargo door closing and first engine start.
Each engine should be started individually at the gate.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added additional (O) guidance to monitor PECS temperature.

ListItem.Para: On POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm SUPPLY TEMP of left and right PECS loop is 142 degrees F (61 degrees C) or lower.
Left AGCU operates normally.
Left ASG generator operates normally.
Left ASG starter operates normally.
APU cannot be started using the APU battery.
ELEC APU START POWER UNIT status message will display during the APU start.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added (O) note to provide further dispatch clarification.

ListItem.Para: On POWER ELEC COOLING maintenance page 1, confirm SUPPLY TEMP of left and right PECS loop is 142 degrees F (61 degrees C) or lower.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One or both may be inoperative.
One or both may be inoperative.
